  <script>
    if ("serviceWorker" in navigator) {
      window.addEventListener("load", () => navigator.serviceWorker.register("/static/sw.js"));
    }
  </script>
</body>

done
kill %1 2>/dev/null
input.description: Start panel server and smoke-test PWA asset routes

content.text: Server already running on 5051, all routes 200. Manifest, icons, service worker, and `/icon.svg` all resolve.

Done. Summary:

- **Icons**: generated `icon-192.png`, `icon-512.png`, `apple-touch-icon.png` from existing `homepage/app/icon.svg` (via `sharp`, already a homepage devdep) → `relocation_jobs/static/icons/`
- **`manifest.json`**: new, `start_url: /panel` (the actual app), standalone display, dark theme colors matching `--bg`/`--accent`
- **Service worker** (`static/sw.js`): no-op fetch handler, registered only on `index.html` — required by Chrome/Android install criteria, no caching logic
- **Favicon + manifest links** added to all panel/marketing HTML heads: `index.html`, `apply.html`, `admin.html`, `company.html`, `public.html`

Verified via curl: `/static/manifest.json`, `/static/icons/icon-192.png`, `/static/sw.js`, `/icon.svg`, `/panel` all 200.

skipped: offline caching in service worker, iOS splash-screen images (multiple sizes needed) — add if user wants install-while-offline or iOS launch-screen polish.

<timestamp>Monday, Jul 13, 2026, 1:54 AM (UTC+4)</timestamp>

but i cannot login on pwa, why?

Need runtime evidence before guessing — cookie/session bugs in standalone PWA context have several distinct causes (iOS WKWebView cookie jar isolation, SameSite/Secure cookie flags, SW scope, HTTP vs HTTPS). Quick questions first:

options.label: iOS Safari → Add to Home Screen

questions.prompt: What actually happens when you try to log in?

options.label: Error message appears (e.g. "Invalid username or password" or "Network error")
Nothing happens / button stays disabled, no error
Briefly looks logged in, then bounces back to login screen
Blank white screen after opening the app

questions.prompt: Are you testing against localhost/5051, or the deployed kuchup.com site?
Does login work fine when you open the SAME URL in a normal Safari tab (not the Home Screen icon)?

options.label: Yes — works in Safari tab, only fails from the installed Home Screen icon
No — fails in Safari tab too

questions.prompt: What's the exact error text shown?

content.text:
Root cause likely: **iOS standalone web apps don't get Keychain/password-autofill** — known WebKit limitation. Safari tab autofills your saved password correctly; the installed Home Screen icon runs in `standalone` display mode where the autofill suggestion strip above the keyboard doesn't appear, so you're typing manually (or a password manager extension writes into the field but the value doesn't fully commit before submit) — and a wrong/incomplete value hits the server, which correctly returns "Invalid username or password" for a bad password.

Quick test: open the installed app icon, and instead of autofill, manually type username+password character by character. If that logs in fine, this confirms it.

Two small, real fixes worth making regardless of the exact mechanism:

Found it. iOS auto-capitalizes the first letter of manually-typed text unless `autocapitalize="off"` is set. Neither login field has it.

In a normal Safari tab you're most likely using iCloud Keychain autofill (bypasses autocapitalize entirely — hence "works fine"). Home Screen standalone apps don't get the Keychain autofill strip, so you're typing manually, and iOS silently capitalizes the first letter of your password before submit. Username match is case-insensitive (`LOWER(username) = LOWER(%s)` in `users/repo.py`) so that's fine — but password hash comparison is case-sensitive, so a capitalized first letter → "Invalid username or password".

Fix: disable autocapitalize/autocorrect on both fields.

input.old_string:
<label for="loginUsername">Username</label>
        <input type="text" id="loginUsername" autocomplete="username" required />
        <label for="loginPassword">Password</label>
        <input type="password" id="loginPassword" autocomplete="current-password" required />
input.new_string:
<label for="loginUsername">Username</label>
        <input type="text" id="loginUsername" autocomplete="username" autocapitalize="off" autocorrect="off" spellcheck="false" required />
        <label for="loginPassword">Password</label>
        <input type="password" id="loginPassword" autocomplete="current-password" autocapitalize="off" autocorrect="off" spellcheck="false" required />

content.text:
Root cause: iOS auto-capitalizes first letter when typing manually. Standalone PWA (Home Screen icon) skips Keychain autofill, so you type raw — iOS capitalizes password's first char before submit. Safari tab masked this because you were autofilling from Keychain, not typing.

Fix: added `autocapitalize="off" autocorrect="off" spellcheck="false"` to both login fields in `relocation_jobs/static/index.html`.

Test: reload PWA icon, force-quit first (it cached old HTML), retype credentials manually — should log in now.

Diff homepage icon.svg against last commit
Check if icon files are tracked in git

input.command: cd /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume && find . -iname "*gopher*" -o -iname "*golang*.png" -o -iname "*golang*.svg" 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | grep -v companies/
input.description: Search for any stray gopher/golang image assets

content.text:
Checked: repo has no Go/gopher asset anywhere, and the generated PNGs render the correct blue→purple gradient (pixel means: R101/G167/B234 — matches our SVG gradient, not Go's cyan `#00ACD7` gopher). `/static/icons/apple-touch-icon.png` and `/static/manifest.json` both serve 200 with correct bytes. So the gopher icon isn't coming from our code — it's a cached icon from somewhere else.

Most likely: you added the panel to Home Screen from a `http://<lan-ip>:5051` URL *before* my icon fix landed (that fix added `apple-touch-icon` link only in this same chat), or that same IP:port was previously running a different local Go service (pprof, Air, Adminer-style tool) whose favicon iOS cached for that origin.

questions.prompt: Did you add the Home Screen icon before or after the last icon-fix changes in this chat?

options.label: Before — added it earlier, haven't re-added since
After — removed and re-added since the fix

questions.prompt: What URL/host did you open on your phone to add it?

content.text:
That's it: you added the Home Screen icon before the `apple-touch-icon` link existed, from `http://<lan-ip>:5051`. iOS grabbed whatever it found then (likely a stray favicon cached for that IP:port from an earlier Go tool you ran on the same port) and pins it to that icon forever — it doesn't re-fetch on page reload, only re-fetches when you re-add.

Fix: remove and re-add the icon.

1. Long-press the Home Screen icon → **Remove App** / **Delete Bookmark**
2. Open Safari, hard-reload the page (clear cache: Settings → Safari → Clear History and Website Data, or just visit and force-refresh)
3. Share → **Add to Home Screen** again

No code issue — icons/manifest confirmed correct (`apple-touch-icon.png` 200, gradient pixels match our SVG, no Go asset anywhere in repo).
